# GitHub repo page setup

Paste these into the repo's **About** panel (the gear icon, top-right of the
repo home page).

## About (description)

> A config-driven, self-managed retirement planning toolkit: a multi-tab
> spreadsheet model, a Monte Carlo engine, an HTML dashboard, and an
> employer-stock health monitor that tracks price, analyst, and SEC-filing
> signals to inform RSU and concentration decisions. Ships with a fully
> de-identified, runnable demo. Educational use only — not financial advice.

(If you need it shorter for the 350-char field:)

> Self-managed retirement planning toolkit — spreadsheet model, Monte Carlo
> engine, dashboard, and an employer-stock health monitor for RSU/concentration
> decisions. Config-driven, with a runnable de-identified demo. Educational
> only; not financial advice.

## Topics / tags

```
retirement-planning
personal-finance
```

## Recommended repo settings
- **Website**: leave blank, or point to the rendered demo dashboard if you host one.
- **Include in the home page**: Releases off, Packages off (none here).
- Add a **CI badge** to the README (already included) so visitors see the demo
  passes on every push: `![CI](https://github.com/<you>/<repo>/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)`
- Consider enabling **Discussions** if you want friends to ask setup questions.
